Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion To Specially Set A Trial Date Pursuant To C.C.P. 36(A) And (D); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Jan-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Hannah B. Oxley of GOLD LAW FIRM (415-986-1338, X-122), appearing for plaintiff and moving party WINSTON NICHOLS and MARLENE NICHOLS
Anna C. Mourlam of GORDON & REES LLP (510-463-8656), appearing for defendant and opposing party THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY
Rochelle R. Ileto of POND NORTH LLP (415-217-1240 / 213-617-6170), appearing for defendant of FMC CORPORATION ON BEHALF OF ITS FORMER PEERLESS PUMP BUSINESS
Joseph B. Adams of BASSI EDLIN HUIE & BLUM (415-397-9006), appearing for defendant PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
Andrea J. Casalett of DENTONS US LLP (415-267-4094), appearing for defendant and joinder in opposition METALCLAD INSULATION LLC
Dean Pollack of BURNHAM BROWN (510-835-6714), appearing for defendant and joinder in opposition York International Corporation
Thomas Steig of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for defendant SYD CARPENTER, MARINE CONTRACTOR, INC., TRANE U.S. INC. F/K/A AMERICAN STANDARD, INC., INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel

RULING:

Hearing held and modifies its ruling as follows: Plaintiffs' motion to specially set a trial date is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Trial date is April 30, 2018 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is May 18, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 26, 2018. The parties stipulate that summary judgment/adjudication motions may be brought on 35 days' notice. Opposition due 10 days before the hearing. Reply due 4 days before the hearing. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than March 16, 2018. Responses to the demand are due no later than March 29, 2018. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by April 20, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on April 25, 2018. Fact discovery cut-off date is April 13, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is April 27, 2018. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than February 16, 2018. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
